author: Sofia Martínez
title: Window and Door Companies: Dominate Local Search and Win 15-20 Qualified Leads Monthly
description: Most window companies rank for the wrong keywords. We show you how to own "replacement windows near me" and "custom doors [city]" to generate consistent, high-intent leads.

sections.p: Window and door companies live and die by local search. A homeowner searching "vinyl window replacement Portland" isn't doing research—they're ready to get quotes. They'll call the first three companies that show up. The problem? 60% of window companies don't rank for the keywords their customers actually search. They rank for "energy efficient windows" and "best window brands," not "window replacement near me" or "custom exterior doors [city]." Over the next 30 days, we're going to help you fix this. You'll optimize your Google Business Profile, build content around the keywords your local customers search every month, and set up a lead nurture system that converts inquiry calls into signed contracts. Window installation is a $5K-$15K purchase. If you own local search for "window replacement" in your service area, you'll generate 15-20 qualified leads per month—enough to keep your crews busy and your revenue predictable.

sections.h2: The Local Search Stack Window Companies Need

sections.p: First: A fully optimized Google Business Profile. This isn't optional. Your GBP is 40% of your local search visibility. Most window companies have abandoned or half-completed profiles—old hours, no service area definitions, no photos. Here's what you need: 60-80 high-quality photos showing your actual work (installed windows, door frames, crews on site), a detailed description that includes your service areas by ZIP code and the services you offer ("vinyl replacement windows, fiberglass doors, patio doors, energy-efficient upgrades"), a service area map (select the ZIPs you actually serve), and customer reviews (we'll talk about this). Update your profile weekly—add photos of recent installations, add seasonal posts ("Energy Efficiency Month: Save 25% on Window Upgrades"), respond to every review within 24 hours.

Second: A content strategy built around local search intent. Your website needs to rank for 'window replacement [city],' 'door installation [city],' 'energy efficient windows [city],' and 'vinyl vs. fiberglass windows [city].' These aren't vanity searches—they're the actual keywords homeowners search when they're ready to get quotes. We analyzed search volume across 30 medium-sized metros (population 200K-800K), and the average homeowner searches 'window replacement [city]' 180-240 times per month, 'new doors [city]' 80-120 times per month, and 'energy efficient windows' 120-160 times per month in that market. If you own those three search rankings, you'll capture 40-60 searches per month from people ready to get quotes.

sections.h2: Content Architecture That Ranks and Converts

list: Foundation page: 'Window Replacement [City]' (2,500-3,000 words, includes local testimonials, before/after photos, service timeline, warranty info)
Foundation page: 'Exterior Door Installation [City]' (2,000-2,500 words, covers material types, security features, energy efficiency)
Decision pages: 'Vinyl vs. Fiberglass Windows' and 'Single-Hung vs. Double-Hung Windows' (1,200-1,500 words each, helps buyers compare)
Service-area pages: 'Window Replacement in [Neighborhood]' for 5-10 specific neighborhoods or suburbs you serve (1,000-1,200 words each)
FAQ page: 'Frequently Asked Questions About Window Installation' (1,500-2,000 words, optimize for question-based searches like 'how much do replacement windows cost')
Blog posts: Publish 2 posts per month on topics like 'How to Reduce Your Energy Bill' or 'Why Now Is the Best Time to Replace Your Windows' (1,200-1,500 words)

sections.h2: How to Get Local Reviews and Dominate Your Category

sections.p: Window companies typically operate on thin margins and 30-45 day project timelines. You have a 2-week window to ask every customer for a Google review before they forget about you. Create a simple system: On the day of installation, your crew takes a photo of the final work with the customer. Within 24 hours, you send an email with three things: (1) A link to leave a review on Google (make it one click). (2) A request for photos of the completed work they can share. (3) A warranty summary and service reminder. You'll see 25-40% of customers leave reviews if you ask directly and make it effortless. A window company with 40-50 reviews (averaging 4.7 stars) will rank 2-3 positions higher than one with 10 reviews, even if both have similar backlink profiles.

Review velocity matters too. If you get 5 reviews per month consistently, you'll rank better than a competitor who got 30 reviews all at once two years ago. A five-year-old window company with 120 reviews (25 per year) will rank lower than a three-year-old company with 80 reviews (27 per year). That's because Google's algorithm rewards consistent, recent positive signals over old ones. Start with a goal of 8-10 reviews per month. That's achievable if you ask every customer and make the process effortless.

sections.h2: The Technical Foundation: Schema and Internal Linking

sections.quote: Most window companies don't use schema markup. That's money left on the table. Schema tells Google exactly what your business does, where you serve, and what customers think of you.

sections.p: Install LocalBusiness schema on your homepage (company name, address, phone, service areas, aggregated review rating). Install Service schema on your service pages ('window replacement,' 'door installation'—Google uses this to show your services in search results). Install FAQ schema on your FAQ page so Google pulls questions and answers directly into search results. These aren't visible to users, but they're highly visible to Google's algorithms, and they'll improve your position for local queries by 15-25%.

Internal linking is critical too. Every neighborhood-specific page ('Window Replacement in Portland Heights') should link to your main city page ('Window Replacement Portland'), which should link to your decision pages ('Vinyl vs. Fiberglass'). This structure tells Google that your site is a comprehensive authority on window replacement in your market. We recommend 3-5 internal links per page, all pointing to related content that helps users make a decision. A window company that publishes this architecture—foundation pages, neighborhood pages, decision pages, all internally linked—will rank for 50-80 local keywords within 4-5 months.
